entry.title: The Sydney Morning Herald: 19 Jun, 2022
entry.summary: The Sydney Morning Herald: 19 Jun, 2022 published at 2022-06-19 03:39AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Cornes calls for 'simple fix' to vexed rule
  • ‘More spectacular than the Daintree’: $56 million rainforest walking track for NSW
  • The Jezabels take no Prisoners in triumphant trip back in time
  • Police operation targets climate activist group Blockade Australia in Blue Mountains
  • There’s elegant charm in this old ballet – but wasn’t it supposed to be funny?
  • Slater coy on Queensland team
  • ‘You find a way to live with it’: Camilla opens up on public criticism
  • NSW to trial shared equity scheme to help nurses, teachers buy homes
  • $3.5b to be spent on Queensland rail projects in state budget
  • Single flu and COVID test coming, but not in time for this flu season
  • 100 kicks in one game - English final shows Wallabies what is coming
  • Koroisau and Trbojevic back in blue as Fittler rings NSW Origin changes
  • Smith seeks ‘balance in his life’, last-touch rule considered
  • NSW housing scheme to help frontline workers 'get a leg up' in property market
  • Activ employees thrown temporary lifeline to keep workshops open
  • Koroisau to join NSW Blues team
  • Defying Putin’s barbarism, Odesa opera house reopens
  • Contemporary photography rising, but would you pay $200K?
  • Joey's pick to replace Wighton for Blues
  • Thomas frustrated with fourth hole at The Country Club
  • Bipartisanship and progressive policy: is this what it feels like to be a Kiwi?
  • Federal government lobbying behind the scenes for Assange’s freedom
  • Anthony Albanese sets out domestic agenda for first sitting of new parliament
  • Best of cartoons, June 19, 2022
  • The latest illustrations from artist Matt Golding
  • Best of cartoons, June 18, 2022
  • COVID-19 jabs for under-fives ‘a couple of months’ away: Cheng
  • City2Murphy: Sydney Queer Irish find their feet in harbour city
  • Performance pay, revamped school hours: Premier flags education reforms
  • Aboriginal flag to fly permanently atop Harbour Bridge by end of year
  • Libs deputy’s failures Ley it on the line
  • Youth homelessness can be overlooked but there is a way out – as Finn’s story proves
  • ‘A very dangerous operation’: Substation engulfed in flames south of Sydney
  • ‘Blight on our state’: NSW announces $100 million for women’s safety
  • After outcry, Boris Johnson defends 24/7 electronic monitoring of asylum seekers
  • The tech essential that just faded away
  • Joe Biden falls off his bike near his Delaware beach home
  • Japan has the lowest COVID death rate in the OECD without mandates. Here’s why
  • Monkey shot, tiger roams streets as toll mounts for cartels’ exotic pets
  • Colbert show staffers arrested at US Capitol after hours
  • Residents in shock as 200 kilogram tiger wanders streets in Mexico
  • Millions of homes under water as floods hit India, Bangladesh
  • From the Archives, 1972: Concorde’s 002 booms into Melbourne
  • Substation fire in Wollongong under control
  • Home births could be publicly funded in Queensland
  • Darwin driver appears to mow down man in hotel car park
  • Walking in two worlds: the Territory kids discovering a new school
  • At 18, I did not see any light for my future. Then I found faith
  • ‘In it together’: How to tackle teacher burnout to avoid mass exodus
  • ‘Not like an essay for school’: Sydney teen published in prestigious medical journal
  • Xavier College under pressure to remove alleged abuser’s name from arts building
  • Lightscape festival to mesmerise at Botanic Gardens
  • Patience and love: the crucial ingredients for childcare reform
  • The surprising offer from Sky News that I had to refuse
  • Prime ministers, popes and princes: a day in the life of a protective service agent
  • Bitcoin drops below US$20,000 as crypto selloff quickens
  • How the cost of your Sunday roast went up almost 40% in three years
  • ‘I bring my sister a bag of lettuce’: The gardeners avoiding outrageous prices
  • Warren Buffett’s final charity lunch draws record $27 million winning bid
  • The Logies: Our guide to who should win, who will win, and why
  • ‘Too tired to keep track’: The struggle of writing a book with a newborn in tow
  • TV sweetheart and former nominee snubbed from Logies
  • Author Anne Tyler: ‘What am I going to do with my life? I’m only 80!’
  • Melbourne’s much-loved community footy match is back, and with $100k to boot
  • The 25 best Australian novels of the last 25 years
  • Why Yassmin Abdel-Magied is a big proponent of the marriage contract
  • ‘I’m not afraid to fail’: Jim Courier on new challenges and hostile crowds
  • National weather forecast for Sunday June 19
  • National weather forecast for Saturday June 18
Breaking news from Sydney, Australia and the world. Features the latest business, sport, entertainment, travel, lifestyle, and technology news.

entry.title: ChannelNewsAsia: 19 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • North Korea deploys national medical teams to battle intestinal epidemic
  • MacDonald upbeat over Blues future despite Super Rugby defeat
  • China's JD.com posts slowest growth ever in '618' shopping event
  • NATO warns of long Ukraine war as Russian assaults follow EU boost for Kyiv
  • Robertson hails 'special' Eden Park success as Crusaders win again
  • Apple workers at Maryland store vote to unionise, a first for the US
  • Malaysian actress and singer Adibah Noor dies of ovarian cancer at age 51
  • Islamic State claims attack on Sikh temple in Kabul that killed two
  • Rahm content despite costly US Open bunker mistake

  • British journalist and Brazilian guide shot in Amazon killings: Police

  • US rolls out COVID-19 vaccine for younger children

  • This polygraph examiner can sniff out lies, with or without equipment. Here’s how he does it

  • China's May gasoline exports plunge, LNG imports slide

  • Pink Dot returns to Hong Lim Park for the first time since COVID-19 pandemic started; MPs among attendees

  • US Open leaders greeted by tough scoring conditions
  • Stormers fight back to win URC title in S.African derby
  • Alonso fastest in wet final practice in Canada
  • More than half of all players at Euros, AFCON finals abused online-study

  • Ferrari's Leclerc to start from the back of grid in Canada
  • Hamilton on a high with fourth in Canada qualifying
  • Mexico City punches its way to a new Guinness record
  • Kyrgios hits 30 aces but loses Halle semi-final to Hurkacz
  • Borthwick restores Leicester's place at the top one game at a time
  • Last-minute Burns drop goal wins Premiership title for Leicester
  • Australia's Winnington cruises to 400m freestyle gold
  • Berrettini powers into Queen's final
  • Brazil police arrest third suspect in murder of British journalist and indigenous expert
  • 'I am good': Biden falls from bike but is unhurt
  • Ukraine president praises troops in visit to southern front line
  • WHO drops endemic country distinction for monkeypox
  • Ukraine's fight grinds on as EU leaders set to grant the country candidate status
  • Iran fighter jet crashes, injuring two crew: Reports
  • UK's Johnson confident of legality of Rwanda migrant plan

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 19 Jun, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 19 Jun, 2022 published at 2022-06-19 03:15AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Beijing approves Hong Kong Chief Executive-elect John Lee’s cabinet line-up
  • Wheelchair-friendly places and foster homes: father’s love conquers life of hard knocks for this Hong Kong family
  • From shoot-outs on Hong Kong streets to online scams raking in billions: police chief Raymond Siu digs into three decades of crime
  • Health of Hong Kong’s poor is in the hands of the whole of society, not just the heathcare sector
  • Masks, Covid-19 test kits, plastic bottles: more than 9,000kg of litter found on Hong Kong’s hiking trails, seashore last year, green group says
  • Fraudsters swindled HK$1.28 billion from more than 6,000 Hongkongers through online, phone scams in first 4 months of year
  • Hong Kong’s buyers snap up 70 per cent of new homes on offer, casting aside HKMA’s 75-point interest hike as banks deferred raising mortgage rates
  • Hong Kong must prepare for uncertainty as US-China tensions will continue, but opportunities are still present as country welcomes American investors: city’s finance chief
  • Greater Bay Area: can China ‘integrate’ Hong Kong into its southern economic powerhouse?
  • Coronavirus: business at Hong Kong bars, pubs falls 60 per cent amid new Covid rapid-test rule, industry leaders say, as city logs 1,160 local cases
  • Coronavirus: 150 Hong Kong passengers in logistics nightmare after Amsterdam airport staff shortfall, tough quarantine rules in city leave them ‘scared, helpless and clueless’
  • Is abortion legal in China, how common is it and why is it controversial?
  • ‘Find a sperm donor’: Chinese doctor shocked to find failed-pregnancy patient is married to son of her paternal aunt
  • Chinese firm’s hopes of resolving royalties row at massive DR Congo copper and cobalt mine hits political hurdle
  • Biden, China’s Xi ‘will talk’ says US president, weighing action on tariffs
  • Beijing is likely to step up its campaign to ‘reunify’ with Taiwan, analysts say
  • China-US rivalry dampens belt and road investment in Latin America and the Caribbean, but strong demand remains
  • Chinese disciplinary watchdog to investigate after bank protesters flagged as health risk
  • Chinese President Xi Jinping warns against international decoupling in Russia speech
  • Biden urges China, other major economies to step up climate action, as US and EU join global methane cuts pledge
  • China to tighten grip on social media comments, requiring sites to employ sufficient content moderators
  • China’s latest hot export is a US$50 toy Russian fighter jet that ‘will never break’, helped by social media such as TikTok
  • Huge blaze at Shanghai Sinopec plant kills one
  • Chinese university student wins Apple’s Swift coding challenge with genetics experiment simulation
  • Coronavirus: which works better to handle a pandemic – democracy or autocracy?
  • Malaysia wants to fight inflation with GST. Will it cost Ismail Sabri at the polls?
  • Australia’s China bias and colonial blinkers mean it fails to see big picture for Pacific islands’ development
  • Will Indonesia’s Jokowi start his own political party to cement his legacy?
  • US, China talks in Luxembourg may set stage for Biden-Xi face-to-face summit
  • Singapore’s Pink Dot gay pride rally returns, as MP from ruling party attends for first time – and in a pink T-shirt
  • ‘Never seen such floods in her life’: 41 dead, millions stranded in India, Bangladesh
  • The way we wed: how Indian brides are reinventing ceremonies to push for gender equality
  • Oil-producing Gulf nations are pursuing greener energy. What does this mean for Asia?
  • Joe Biden takes a spill while dismounting after bike ride near the US president’s holiday home
  • Warplanes hit Hamas sites in Gaza in retaliation to rocket fire, Israeli army says
  • Spain battles to bring fires under control as it swelters in heatwave
  • Defrocked Chilean priest sentenced to 15 years in prison for sexually abusing minors
  • Ukraine plans to resume peace talks with Russia by August, officials say
  • Coronavirus: vaccine roll-out for American children under 5 close after CDC panel vote
  • ‘Bloodsicles’ and ice baths: How US zookeepers coax animals through heatwaves
  • Brazil police arrest third suspect in Amazon shooting deaths
  • Kate Bush classic Running Up That Hill tops UK chart after 37 years
  • Gunman, 70, kills 3 senior citizens during potluck dinner at Alabama church
  • US billionaire Warren Buffett’s last charity private lunch sees record US$19 million bid
  • Russia frees Ukraine medic who filmed Mariupol horror, saved both sides’ people
  • Boris sings out for Ukraine: UK PM Johnson says nation deserves to host Eurovision
  • UK begins electronically tagging migrants coming via ‘unnecessary, dangerous routes’
  • Woman arrested for making anti-Asian remarks, pepper spraying 4 people in New York
  • Ukraine war: captured US fighters Alexander Drueke and Andy Huynh surface in Russian state TV videos
  • What makes the e-CNY different from bitcoin? Central bank digital currencies share little with cryptocurrencies
  • Bitcoin’s cascading losses accelerate in record-breaking rout
  • Post-lockdown Shanghai among top spending cities during China’s 618 online shopping extravaganza
  • ByteDance closes Shanghai games studio in retreat from industry dominated by Tencent, NetEase
  • 6 Hong Kong heritage and historical buildings that dodged demolition, and 6 that did not, since 1997
  • Like Queen Elizabeth, China’s Kangxi Emperor had a long and glorious reign; he did start younger than her, though, at the age of seven
  • When Hong Kong was a hotbed of high-speed smuggling and nautical shoot-outs, dubbed ‘Chicago-on-the-Sea’ as public alarm grew
  • K-drama midseason recap: Kiss Sixth Sense – Disney+’s muddled fantasy romance is seriously in need of an 11th-hour rescue
  • Maggie Cheung in her own words, on Wong Kar-wai and In the Mood for Love, marrying Olivier Assayas and being an actress, not just a film star
  • Sweet Home on Netflix: seasons 2 and 3, new stars for monster K-drama series
  • ‘He shared with me his experience with stress – that was really helpful’: why men should face mental health issues head on and talk about them
  • Why sommeliers hate it when you BYOB – a Hong Kong insider explains why
  • Taylor Swift then and now: 8 things you might not know about the singer, from her early financial adviser dreams to why she feels like an outsider
  • Traditional artisans front and centre of tourism promotion in Hanoi as Vietnamese capital seeks to preserve dying craftsman communities
  • Who are the richest Fast & Furious stars? Net worths, ranked – from Dwayne ‘The Rock’ Johnson and Vin Diesel, to Wonder Woman’s Gal Gadot and the late Paul Walker, here’s who raked in millions
  • Meet Elon Musk’s sister Tosca, founder of Netflix-style streaming service Passionflix, accomplished filmmaker and mother of twins – but did her brother invest in her company?
  • 13 little-known facts about Lightyear star Chris Evans, from crushing on Sandra Bullock and matching Avengers tattoos with Scarlett Johansson, to starring in a Marilyn Manson music video
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

entry.content:

Breaking news and analysis on politics, business, world national news, entertainment more. In-depth DC, Virginia, Maryland news coverage including traffic, weather, crime, education, restaurant reviews and more.

entry.content:
Articles in this issue:

entry.title: Asahi Shimbun: 19 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:

  • VOX POPULI: ‘Gerrymanders’ not visible on Japan’s redrawn electoral map
The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.

entry.title: Korea Herald: 19 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Canadian Korean War veteran to be laid to rest in Busan UN cemetery

entry.title: NY Times (Print): 18 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • A Defiant Putin Says Russia Will Flourish Without the West
  • Russians Breached This City, Not With Troops, but Propaganda
  • Amid Jan. 6 Revelations, Election Lies Still Dominate the G.O.P.
  • The Watergate Hearings, 50 Years Ago: Truth Was Not Up for Debate
  • Uterine Cancer Is on the Rise, Especially Among Black Women
  • The Coin That Could Wreck Crypto
  • ‘He Took Jan. 6 Personally’
  • Colombia Will Soon Have Its First Black Female Vice President. Will It Be Her?
  • In These New Textbooks, Hong Kong Was Never a British Colony
  • An ugly week for stocks leaves the S&P 500 with its biggest loss since March 2020.
  • Infighting Overshadows Big Plans at The Washington Post
  • A Decade After DACA, the Rise of a New Generation of Undocumented Students
  • A Gull Flaps Its Wings and a Deadly Virus Explodes
  • Stephen Curry Left His Critics With Nothing Else to Say
  • Rafael Nadal, Chasing Grand Slam, Will Head to Wimbledon
  • ‘The Old Man’ Brought Jeff Bridges to TV. John Lithgow Had No Advice.
  • New York Philharmonic Chooses Arts Veteran as Leader
  • The Rush to E.S.G., With or Without Elon Musk
  • Covid Vaccine and Fisheries Deals Close a ‘Roller Coaster’ W.T.O. Meeting
  • BTS Ponders Its Future, and South Korea’s Economy Warily Takes Note
  • SpaceX Said to Fire Employees Involved in Letter Rebuking Elon Musk
  • With Funding Stalled, Chip Makers Warn Congress the U.S. Is Lagging
  • Why Some States Are Expanding Tax-Free Periods
  • My Father Makes Me So Angry. I Take Care of Him Anyway.
  • Crypto, Houses, Sneakers, Rolexes: How FOMO Drove the Economy
  • Pregnancy Can Affect the Body Forever. Have Abortion Foes Reckoned With That?
  • Something Extraordinary Is Happening in France
  • We Should Thank Mike Pence for His Courage on Jan. 6
  • A Filmmaker Imagines a Japan Where the Elderly Volunteer to Die
  • China Launches Third Aircraft Carrier in Major Milestone for Xi Jinping
  • A High-Flying Italian Surgeon’s Fall From Grace
  • On the edge of embattled twin cities, an uncovered mass grave for civilians grows.
  • Torched Trains and Burning Tires: India’s New Military Recruitment Plan Fuels Angry Protests
  • ‘Wholly Unexpected’: These Polar Bears Can Survive With Less Sea Ice
  • She Spoke Out Against Vietnam’s Plans for Coal. Then She Was Arrested.
  • Fuel-Starved and Stuck in Crisis, Sri Lanka Orders Work from Home
  • Man Paralyzed 12 Years Ago Becomes Italy’s First Assisted Suicide
  • Greek Court Rejects Effort to Toughen Punishment After Killing of an American
  • Jan. 6 Panel Could Start Sharing Transcripts With Justice Dept. as Soon as July
  • A Day After a Portrait of Pence in Danger, Trump Attacks Him Again
  • A man who threatened Colorado’s secretary of state on Instagram faces two years in prison.
  • A Better Way to Measure Immunity in Children
  • F.D.A. Authorizes Moderna and Pfizer Covid Vaccines for Youngest Children
  • N.Y.C.’s vaccine plan for children younger than 5 relies on pediatricians and vaccine hubs.
  • Fast-Tracked Bill Stokes Fears of Private Development in 1,200-Acre Park
  • The Jury in the Bill Cosby Sex Assault Case Says It’s Close to a Verdict
  • New York City Estimates More Unsheltered People, but Comparisons Are Tricky
  • Uvalde Officer Passed Up Shot at Gunman for Fear of Hitting Children
  • Police Error Led to Trump-Era Subpoena to Twitter About a Devin Nunes Critic
  • N.J. Transit Shuts Down Train Service, Blaming Engineer Sickout
  • Jean-Louis Trintignant, Star of Celebrated European Films, Dies at 91
  • Tim Sale, Comic Book Artist Who Reimagined Batman, Dies at 66
